item.text: {"approval_state":"proposed","bottleneck":"The 2x2 CUDA probe matrix is now structurally complete and each cell has a matching rerun, but the workflow has not yet converted those four configuration-specific claims into one conservative matrix-level analysis, so the system still lacks a reviewed statement about whether the stride penalty persists at both working-set sizes and how large the effect appears.","continue_recommended":true,"expected_gain":"A single matrix-analysis step can turn four verified point measurements into one higher-value but still narrow conclusion, while preserving append-only history and avoiding unnecessary new benchmark execution before the current evidence is fully exploited.","proposal":"Run one rollbackable analysis-only step in `workflow_v1` that consumes the four accepted, verified cells in the completed 2x2 matrix (`working_set_kb` in {2048, 256}, `stride_bytes` in {128, 4}) and produces a matrix-level comparison artifact. Require the analyzer to compute only matched ratios/deltas already supported by accepted evidence: stride effect at 2048 KB, stride effect at 256 KB, working-set effect at 128-byte stride, and working-set effect at 4-byte stride. Permit a narrow claim only if it stays descriptive and tied to this probe family, for example that 4-byte stride measured substantially higher bandwidth than 128-byte stride at both tested working-set sizes under identical launch parameters; keep cache-level or causal attribution out of curated knowledge unless later evidence is added. Preserve append-only history, keep benchmark family and single-GPU scope unchanged, and route any resulting matrix-level claim through the normal verification stage before curation.","proposed_profile_updates":[{"context_sections":["question","spec","claim","verification"],"instruction_note":"When all declared cells of a controlled matrix are complete, require the analyzer to emit a fixed comparison table listing each matched ratio/delta it computed and the exact claim ids used for each comparison.","reason":"This makes matrix-level reasoning auditable for human review and prevents silent cross-run mixing.","role":"analyzer","sandbox_mode":"read-only","web_search_enabled":false},{"context_sections":["claim","verification"],"instruction_note":"For curated knowledge, allow matrix-level summary claims only when every contributing cell has both an accepted claim and an accepted independent rerun; otherwise keep outputs at per-configuration granularity.","reason":"This preserves rigor when elevating from single-cell observations to comparative conclusions.","role":"curator","sandbox_mode":"read-only","web_search_enabled":false}],"risk":"Because each cell still comes from limited repeat counts and single reruns, a matrix-level analysis may overstate stability if phrased too strongly; the main failure mode is comparative wording that outruns the evidence rather than a workflow invariant breach.","rollback_condition":"If the analyzer cannot map all four comparisons to accepted claim ids with matched benchmark metadata, or if human review finds the matrix-level wording causal, cache-specific, or broader than the verified evidence supports, discard the matrix-level artifact and retain only the existing per-cell accepted claims and verifications.","stop_reason":null,"target_workflow_version":"workflow_v1"}
